children.heading: Trade and business deductions
children.label: 1 Trade and business deductions
children.text: The deductions allowed by this chapter (other than by part VII of this subchapter) which are attributable to a trade or business carried on by the taxpayer, if such trade or business does not consist of the performance of services by the taxpayer as an employee.

children.heading: Certain trade and business deductions of employees
children.label: 2 Certain trade and business deductions of employees

children.heading: Reimbursed expenses of employees
children.label: A Reimbursed expenses of employees
children.text: The deductions allowed by part VI (section 161 and following) which consist of expenses paid or incurred by the taxpayer, in connection with the performance by him of services as an employee, under a reimbursement or other expense allowance arrangement with his employer. The fact that the reimbursement may be provided by a third party shall not be determinative of whether or not the preceding sentence applies.

children.heading: Certain expenses of performing artists
children.label: B Certain expenses of performing artists
children.text: The deductions allowed by section 162 which consist of expenses paid or incurred by a qualified performing artist in connection with the performances by him of services in the performing arts as an employee.

children.heading: Certain expenses of officials
children.label: C Certain expenses of officials
children.text: The deductions allowed by section 162 which consist of expenses paid or incurred with respect to services performed by an official as an employee of a State or a political subdivision thereof in a position compensated in whole or in part on a fee basis.

children.heading: Certain expenses of elementary and secondary school teachers
children.label: D Certain expenses of elementary and secondary school teachers
i
children.text: by reason of the participation of the educator in professional development courses related to the curriculum in which the educator provides instruction or to the students for which the educator provides instruction, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in connection with books, supplies (other than nonathletic supplies for courses of instruction in health or physical education), computer equipment (including related software and services) and other equipment, and supplementary materials used by the eligible educator in the classroom.

children.heading: Certain expenses of members of reserve components of the Armed Forces of the United States
children.label: E Certain expenses of members of reserve components of the Armed Forces of the United States
children.text: The deductions allowed by section 162 which consist of expenses, determined at a rate not in excess of the rates for travel expenses (including per diem in lieu of subsistence) authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, United States Code, paid or incurred by the taxpayer in connection with the performance of services by such taxpayer as a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces of the United States for any period during which such individual is more than 100 miles away from home in connection with such services.

children.heading: Losses from sale or exchange of property
children.label: 3 Losses from sale or exchange of property
children.text: The deductions allowed by part VI (sec. 161 and following) as losses from the sale or exchange of property.

children.heading: Deductions attributable to rents and royalties
children.label: 4 Deductions attributable to rents and royalties
children.text: The deductions allowed by part VI (sec. 161 and following), by section 212 (relating to expenses for production of income), and by section 611 (relating to depletion) which are attributable to property held for the production of rents or royalties.

children.heading: Certain deductions of life tenants and income beneficiaries of property
children.label: 5 Certain deductions of life tenants and income beneficiaries of property
children.text: In the case of a life tenant of property, or an income beneficiary of property held in trust, or an heir, legatee, or devisee of an estate, the deduction for depreciation allowed by section 167 and the deduction allowed by section 611.

children.heading: Pension, profit-sharing, and annuity plans of self-employed individuals
children.label: 6 Pension, profit-sharing, and annuity plans of self-employed individuals
children.text: In the case of an individual who is an employee within the meaning of section 401(c)(1), the deduction allowed by section 404.

children.heading: Retirement savings
children.label: 7 Retirement savings
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 219 (relating to deduction of certain retirement savings).

children.heading: Penalties forfeited because of premature withdrawal of funds from time savings accounts or deposits
children.label: 9 Penalties forfeited because of premature withdrawal of funds from time savings accounts or deposits
children.text: The deductions allowed by section 165 for losses incurred in any transaction entered into for profit, though not connected with a trade or business, to the extent that such losses include amounts forfeited to a bank, mutual savings bank, savings and loan association, building and loan association, cooperative bank or homestead association as a penalty for premature withdrawal of funds from a time savings account, certificate of deposit, or similar class of deposit.

children.heading: Reforestation expenses
children.label: 11 Reforestation expenses
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 194.

children.heading: Certain required repayments of supplemental unemployment compensation benefits
children.label: 12 Certain required repayments of supplemental unemployment compensation benefits
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 165 for the repayment to a trust described in paragraph (9) or (17) of section 501(c) of supplemental unemployment compensation benefits received from such trust if such repayment is required because of the receipt of trade readjustment allowances under section 231 or 232 of the Trade Act of 1974 ( and 2292).19 U.S.C. 2291

children.heading: Jury duty pay remitted to employer
children.label: 13 Jury duty pay remitted to employer
children.text: Any deduction allowable under this chapter by reason of an individual remitting any portion of any jury pay to such individual’s employer in exchange for payment by the employer of compensation for the period such individual was performing jury duty. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “jury pay” means any payment received by the individual for the discharge of jury duty.

children.label: 14 Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 113–295, div. A, title II, § 221(a)(34)(C)128 Stat. 4042Dec. 19, 2014

children.heading: Moving expenses
children.label: 15 Moving expenses
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 217.

children.heading: Archer MSAs
children.label: 16 Archer MSAs
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 220.

children.heading: Interest on education loans
children.label: 17 Interest on education loans
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 221.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 116–260, div. EE, title I, § 104(b)(2)(A)134 Stat. 3041Dec. 27, 2020
children.label: 18 Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 116–260, div. EE, title I, § 104(b)(2)(A)134 Stat. 3041Dec. 27, 2020

children.heading: Health savings accounts
children.label: 19 Health savings accounts
children.text: The deduction allowed by section 223.

children.heading: Costs involving discrimination suits, etc.
children.label: 20 Costs involving discrimination suits, etc.
children.text: Any deduction allowable under this chapter for attorney fees and court costs paid by, or on behalf of, the taxpayer in connection with any action involving a claim of unlawful discrimination (as defined in subsection (e)) or a claim of a violation of subchapter III of chapter 37 of title 31, United States Code, or a claim made under section 1862(b)(3)(A) of the Social Security Act (). The preceding sentence shall not apply to any deduction in excess of the amount includible in the taxpayer’s gross income for the taxable year on account of a judgment or settlement (whether by suit or agreement and whether as lump sum or periodic payments) resulting from such claim.42 U.S.C. 1395y(b)(3)(A)

children.heading: Attorneys’ fees relating to awards to whistleblowers
children.label: 21 Attorneys’ fees relating to awards to whistleblowers
i
children.text: section 7623(b), or

children.label: I
children.text: section 21F of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (),15 U.S.C. 78u–6

children.label: II
children.text: a State false claims act, including a State false claims act with qui tam provisions, or

children.label: III
children.text: section 23 of the Commodity Exchange Act ().7 U.S.C. 26

children.heading: May not exceed award
children.label: B May not exceed award
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any deduction in excess of the amount includible in the taxpayer’s gross income for the taxable year on account of such award.

children.label: A
children.text: such individual performed services in the performing arts as an employee during the taxable year for at least 2 employers,

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount allowable as a deduction under section 162 in connection with the performance of such services exceeds 10 percent of such individual’s gross income attributable to the performance of such services, and

children.label: C
children.text: the adjusted gross income of such individual for the taxable year (determined without regard to subsection (a)(2)(B)) does not exceed $16,000.

children.heading: Nominal employer not taken into account
children.label: 2 Nominal employer not taken into account
children.text: An individual shall not be treated as performing services in the performing arts as an employee for any employer during any taxable year unless the amount received by such individual from such employer for the performance of such services during the taxable year equals or exceeds $200.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except in the case of a husband and wife who lived apart at all times during the taxable year, if the taxpayer is married at the close of the taxable year, subsection (a)(2)(B) shall apply only if the taxpayer and his spouse file a joint return for the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: paragraph (1) (other than subparagraph (C) thereof) shall be applied separately with respect to each spouse, but

children.label: ii
children.text: paragraph (1)(C) shall be applied with respect to their combined adjusted gross income.

children.heading: Determination of marital status
children.label: C Determination of marital status
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, marital status shall be determined under section 7703(a).

children.heading: Joint return
children.label: D Joint return
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “joint return” means the joint return of a husband and wife made under section 6013.

children.heading: Certain arrangements not treated as reimbursement arrangements
children.label: c Certain arrangements not treated as reimbursement arrangements

children.text: such arrangement does not require the employee to substantiate the expenses covered by the arrangement to the person providing the reimbursement, or
such arrangement provides the employee the right to retain any amount in excess of the substantiated expenses covered under the arrangement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(2)(D), the term “eligible educator” means, with respect to any taxable year, an individual who is a kindergarten through grade 12 teacher, instructor, counselor, principal, or aide in a school for at least 900 hours during a school year.

children.heading: School
children.label: B School
children.text: The term “school” means any school which provides elementary education or secondary education (kindergarten through grade 12), as determined under State law.

children.heading: Coordination with exclusions
children.label: 2 Coordination with exclusions
children.text: A deduction shall be allowed under subsection (a)(2)(D) for expenses only to the extent the amount of such expenses exceeds the amount excludable under section 135, 529(c)(1), or 530(d)(2) for the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2014” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.
